title: Valentines Day

jokes.text: Like last year, Jim gave me this card with Dwight's head on it. It was horrifying and funny.
jokes.setup: Pam talking about Valentine's Day gifts in the office
jokes.punchline: Jim's disturbing but funny Dwight card from last year
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Jim's pranks on Dwight are core to the show. Nice juxtaposition of horrifying/funny.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Jim gave me this card last Valentine's Day. It's a picture of Dwight on the front.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: And inside it says, 'I'm not superstitious, but I am a little stitious... about you.'

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: But the really disturbing part is that Jim drew Dwight's face to look exactly like him, but with like, really intense eyes staring right at you.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's hilarious but also kind of creepy. I don't know why he thought that was romantic.

jokes.text: Happy Valentine's Day, Darling, love Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration
jokes.setup: Reading a Valentine's card
jokes.punchline: Bob Vance always includes his business name even in romantic messages
jokes.explanation: Excellent running gag that's become iconic. Perfect character consistency and very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: "From Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration."

jokes.text: And you want me to come with you. - No. Opposite of that.
jokes.setup: Dwight assumes Michael wants him to come to New York
jokes.punchline: Michael's blunt 'opposite of that' rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Michael's bluntness vs Dwight's eagerness is classic dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: So you want me to come to New York with you?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No. Do the opposite of that.

jokes.text: Screwed. What is your problem?
jokes.setup: Michael asking about seeing Jan, Dwight knowing they 'you know...'
jokes.punchline: Dwight bluntly saying 'screwed' when Michael trails off
jokes.explanation: Dwight's social obliviousness creates perfect cringe moment. Great character work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So you know that Jan and I have been...

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Screwed.

jokes.text: Her words, not mine. She sent me an e-mail this morning.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he'd have to be a raving lunatic to talk to Jan
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jan literally called him a raving lunatic in an email
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - we think he's being self-aware, then learn it's literal. Perfect Michael obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'd have to be a raving lunatic to talk to Jan.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Jan just sent me an email calling me a raving lunatic.

jokes.text: It's New York, city of love.
jokes.setup: Michael dismissing romance on business trip
jokes.punchline: Dwight calling New York 'city of love' (wrong city)
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight malapropism. He's thinking of Paris but says NY with complete confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not going to New York to fall in love. I'm going for business.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's right. New York is the city of love. You'll be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's Paris, Dwight.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: False. New York. It's right there in the song.

jokes.text: Fuggit abat it!
jokes.setup: Michael bragging about owning New York City
jokes.punchline: His terrible fake New York accent
jokes.explanation: Michael's try-hard NYC impression is very in character but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I own New York City. I basically run the place. *adopts terrible accent* 'Ey, fuggedaboutit! I'm walkin' here! I'm walkin' here!' That's how they talk there.

jokes.text: Manhattan is the other name.
jokes.setup: New York, the city so nice they named it twice
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks Manhattan is the 'other name' for New York
jokes.explanation: Michael's confident wrongness is classic. He completely misunderstands the famous saying.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: New York, the city so nice they named it twice

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, you mean Manhattan? That's the other name for New York.

jokes.text: And I'll end up winning a lot of money, because, they're idiots.
jokes.setup: Jim talking about his Valentine's Day poker game plans
jokes.punchline: Casually calling his friends idiots
jokes.explanation: Jim's casual meanness delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, so I'm gonna go play poker with the guys on Valentine's Day. I mean, what else am I gonna do? Spend time with my wife? That's what idiots do.

jokes.text: Happy Valentine's Day.
jokes.setup: Mystery gift on Dwight's desk, Dwight interrogating Jim about it
jokes.punchline: It's a bobblehead of Dwight himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim prank - thoughtful, weird, and plays to Dwight's ego while being absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Did you leave this on my desk?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Maybe.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Open it.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's a bobblehead of me.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, I thought you'd appreciate having a little you to keep you company at your desk.

jokes.text: It's me. I'm the bobble head.
jokes.setup: Dwight discovering the bobblehead
jokes.punchline: His delighted realization and acceptance
jokes.explanation: Dwight's genuine joy at being made into a bobblehead is perfect character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A bobblehead? Of me?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: This is the greatest gift I have ever received.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm going to treasure this forever.

jokes.text: Yes!
jokes.setup: Dwight realizing he's the bobblehead
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic celebration
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight joy - he's genuinely thrilled by the attention.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Wait... I'm the bobblehead? I'm the bobblehead! Yes! Finally! This is the greatest day of my life!

jokes.text: And I'm gonna go get me a New York slice.
jokes.setup: Michael at his 'favorite New York pizza joint'
jokes.punchline: He's clearly at a tourist trap, not a local spot
jokes.explanation: Michael's tourist behavior while pretending to be a local is classic self-delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I love this place. It's my favorite New York pizza joint. I come here every time I'm in the city.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: The pizza here is authentic. Real New York style.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: See? Look at all these locals.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait... why is everyone wearing 'I ❤️ NY' shirts?

jokes.text: Ryan, what took, you so long? I mean, I just said it to him. Can you believe that?
jokes.setup: Kelly telling Jim about hooking up with Ryan
jokes.punchline: She literally said out loud what she was thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - no filter between thought and speech, creating natural cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: She literally said out loud what she was thinking

jokes.text: I hooked up with her on February 13th.
jokes.setup: Kelly gushing about their romantic hookup to Jim
jokes.punchline: Ryan's clinical, unemotional talking head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition between Kelly's romantic fantasy and Ryan's cold reality. Perfect timing and character contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Kelly and I are not in a relationship. We hooked up once. She's been overestimating the significance of the encounter.

jokes.text: Times Square. Named for all the good times you have when you're in it.
jokes.setup: Michael being a tour guide
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong etymology delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Michael's confident fake facts are classic. The logic is absurd but delivered seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So this building was named after the founder, William Dunder. 'Dunder' is actually an ancient Greek word meaning 'place of paper.' And Mifflin? That's Old English for 'the best.' So Dunder Mifflin literally means 'the best place of paper.' Pretty cool, right?

jokes.text: We have Bubba Gump Shrimp, Red Lobster down there. This is the heart of civilization right here.
jokes.setup: Michael describing great NYC restaurants
jokes.punchline: He lists chain restaurants as the pinnacle of civilization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - no understanding of what makes NYC special, defaults to chain restaurants.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, New York has some of the greatest restaurants in the world. I'm talking about the real five-star establishments that define fine dining.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Olive Garden, Red Lobster, Applebee's... these are the pinnacles of civilization.

jokes.text: There's a guy pooping in a cardboard box down there.
jokes.setup: Michael praising the subway system
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by gross reality
jokes.explanation: Good timing - Michael's tourist enthusiasm immediately deflated by NYC reality.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: The subway system in New York is actually a marvel of modern engineering. It's clean, it's efficient, it's a real testament to urban planning.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What is that smell? Oh my god, is that a rat?

jokes.text: Founded of course, by Theodore Rockefeller.
jokes.setup: Michael giving facts about Rockefeller Center
jokes.punchline: Wrong first name (it's John D. Rockefeller)
jokes.explanation: Another Michael fake fact, but less creative than others.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Did you know Rockefeller Center was built by David Rockefeller?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Actually, it was John D. Rockefeller.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what I said. David.

jokes.text: That's Tina Fey from Saturday Night Live... Oh, I'm sorry. I thought you were... She looked like... she looked a lot like Tina Fey.
jokes.setup: Michael excitedly spotting a celebrity
jokes.punchline: It's not Tina Fey, awkward encounter with random woman
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael cringe - his desperation to spot celebrities leads to social awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that... Tina Fey?

dialogue.speaker: Random Woman
dialogue.line: No, I'm not Tina Fey.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Right, yeah, I knew that. I was just... testing you.

jokes.text: Come on! No, are you s... When? When I was talking to the fake Tina Fey?
jokes.setup: Michael missing some celebrity while bothering fake Tina Fey
jokes.punchline: His realization he missed the real celebrity while harassing a civilian
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the celebrity spotting bit. Michael's priorities are perfectly wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're not... you're not the real Tina Fey?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: No, I'm not.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God. I've been... this whole time I thought you were... and you're just a regular person.

jokes.text: I didn't get anything for Valentine's Day. Oh, I bet you will before the day is over.
jokes.setup: Angela complaining about no Valentine's gifts
jokes.punchline: Dwight's mysterious confidence she'll get something
jokes.explanation: Sets up Dwight's later gift planning. Good character work with Angela's passive-aggressive complaint.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I can't believe I didn't get a single Valentine's Day gift. Not one!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Oh, you'll get something. Trust me.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: How do you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Because I'm going to give you one.

jokes.text: Scranton is great, but New York is like Scranton on acid. No, on speed. Nah. On steroids.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing Scranton to New York
jokes.punchline: Can't decide which drug metaphor works, cycles through them
jokes.explanation: Michael's thought process in real time. His inability to commit to a metaphor is very him.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: New York is like a drug. Actually, Scranton is like a drug. No wait, New York is like a drug you take once and it's amazing. Scranton is like a drug you take every day and it becomes part of your life. Actually, both are like heroin.

jokes.text: It's like Michael Jordan in the NBA finals, or like Stormin' Norman Schwarzkopf. And this presentation is Desert Storm. And soon as it's over, we will not have to deal with those Iraqis anymore.
jokes.setup: Michael describing the pressure he's under
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate war metaphor comparing his presentation to Desert Storm
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate metaphors are classic. Comparing CFO meeting to military operation is absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, this presentation is like Desert Storm. I'm going in, I'm taking fire from all sides, and I'm not coming out until I've secured the oil—I mean, the sale.

jokes.text: Man, that thing's bigger than I am. - No, it's not. - Oh, zip it.
jokes.setup: Kevin commenting on Phyllis's giant Valentine's arrangement
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's defensive response and Kevin's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Decent character interaction. Kevin's bluntness vs Phyllis's sensitivity.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Wow, that's a huge arrangement. Bob must really love you.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: He does. Very much.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he better. That's gotta cost like... a lot of money.

jokes.text: You have been kicked out of every strip club in Albany. - Is that true? - Guilty, yeah.
jokes.setup: Josh introducing Craig to Michael
jokes.punchline: Craig's proud admission of being banned from strip clubs
jokes.explanation: Establishes Craig as unprofessional. Good character introduction through inappropriate detail.

dialogue.speaker: Josh
dialogue.line: Michael, this is Craig. Craig, this is Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I've been banned from every strip club in the city.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Really? How does that even happen?

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm very proud of that accomplishment.

jokes.text: You mean, like a ham? - No. Not like a ham.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining Valentine's gifts should be about the gesture
jokes.punchline: Dwight's completely literal interpretation - a ham as a gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - completely misses the point in the most literal way possible. Highly quotable and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Valentine's Day gifts should be about the gesture, not the price tag.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I understand. I'm giving Erin a ham.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: A ham? Why a ham?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's the gesture of giving. A ham is a generous gift. It shows I care.

jokes.text: She's the worst. - Well, she's our boss. - She ain't my boss, dude. I don't work for that bitch.
jokes.setup: Craig complaining about Jan
jokes.punchline: Michael defending Jan, Craig's vulgar escalation
jokes.explanation: Sets up Michael's awkward position. Craig's unprofessionalism contrasts with Michael's unexpected loyalty.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Jan is the worst. She's so annoying.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Jan is a brilliant businesswoman. She's smart, she's driven—

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Yeah, well she can go f*** herself.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, that's unnecessary.

jokes.text: Maybe because she's my girlfriend. Was, or not my girlfriend. She's... we hooked up and...
jokes.setup: Michael defending Jan against Craig's insults
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals their relationship, then fumbles the explanation
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment. Michael's defensive reaction backfires spectacularly, creates huge problem.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! You can't talk to her like that. She's my... she's my...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: ...my boss's ex-girlfriend's... no wait, she's my...

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Look, the point is, Jan is a very important person and I won't have you disrespecting her.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Wait, why do you care so much?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Because we're... because I... because respect is important in the workplace, Craig.

jokes.text: Soda. Cool.
jokes.setup: Kelly talking about dating Ryan forever, Ryan clearly uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Ryan's minimal response, conversation dies
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkwardness. Ryan's complete disengagement while Kelly plans their future.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about our future together. Like, we could get married, have kids, the whole thing. I'm literally so in love with you.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Yeah... cool.

jokes.text: I know that it's Valentine's Day or whatever, but there's totally no pressure at all of any kind, whatsoever.
jokes.setup: Kelly asking Ryan out
jokes.punchline: Says no pressure while clearly putting massive pressure on him
jokes.explanation: Classic Kelly - says opposite of what she means. The 'or whatever' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: So Ryan, do you want to go out with me? No pressure or anything.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Um, I don't know...

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: Okay, well everyone in the office thinks we're going to get married and have babies, and I've already picked out your wedding ring, so...

jokes.text: And, well, it is Michael. So... yeah, I'm very nervous.
jokes.setup: Jan explaining why she's nervous about Michael's presentation
jokes.punchline: Her resigned acceptance that Michael will probably screw up
jokes.explanation: Jan's weary understanding of Michael's incompetence. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I'm nervous about Michael's presentation.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: He's going to screw it up, isn't he?

jokes.text: What is a business? Is it a collection of numbers and sales reports? Sure. But as you know, David and Jan, it is much more.
jokes.setup: Michael starting his business presentation
jokes.punchline: Philosophical opening instead of actual business data
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - avoids actual work with pretentious nonsense. Sets up the terrible presentation.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking a lot about what it means to be successful. Is it the money? Is it the power? Or is it something deeper, something more meaningful?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Because at the end of the day, we're all just trying to find our place in this world, you know?

jokes.text: Yeah, I shot a bunch of footage around the office, edited it together on my Mac. Was thinking about entering it in some festivals. Probably won't. You know, not what this is about.
jokes.setup: Michael made a movie for his business presentation
jokes.punchline: Thinks it's festival-worthy, then pretends that's not why he made it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - thinks his workplace video is art. The false modesty makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: This film could win awards at Sundance, Toronto, maybe even Cannes.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But that's not why I made it. I made it for the business.

jokes.text: What it's like to walk a mile in Oscar's shoes. Or try on Phyllis' pants.
jokes.setup: Michael's inspirational video narration
jokes.punchline: Weird specific examples that make no sense
jokes.explanation: Absurd metaphors that make no literal sense. Very Michael to think this is profound.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Sometimes you need to take a step back and think about what really matters in life. Like, if you're stuck in traffic, don't honk. Instead, think about penguins. Penguins don't honk. Or if you're at the grocery store and they're out of your favorite cereal, just buy seventeen boxes of a different cereal. That's what I do. And if someone doesn't believe in you, just remember: I once taught my goldfish to play chess. Well, not really. But I told people I did, and now everyone respects me.

jokes.text: Great Scott!
jokes.setup: Michael finishing his presentation
jokes.punchline: Pun on his own last name as conclusion
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but very Michael to think it's clever. The confidence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who's it from? - My mom.
jokes.setup: Oscar getting Valentine's Day delivery
jokes.punchline: It's from his mom, not a romantic partner
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Oscar's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Oh, flowers! Finally, someone remembered me on Valentine's Day.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Who's it from?

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: My mom.

jokes.text: Is it okay if I invite Ryan?
jokes.setup: Jim inviting Kelly to poker to help her move on from Ryan
jokes.punchline: She immediately asks to bring Ryan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - completely misses the point of Jim's advice. Great timing after his pep talk.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So we're gonna play poker on Friday night. You should come.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! That sounds so fun. Can I bring Ryan?

jokes.text: I was under the impression this was more of, like, a meet-and-greet type of deal.
jokes.setup: CFO asking Craig about his financial presentation
jokes.punchline: Craig had no idea what a 'financial presentation' meant
jokes.explanation: Craig's incompetence mirrors Michael's but is less charming. Good contrast setup.

dialogue.speaker: CFO
dialogue.line: Craig, where's your financial presentation?

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what's a financial presentation?

jokes.text: Maybe I should've slept with you too.
jokes.setup: Craig comparing his situation to Michael's movie presentation
jokes.punchline: Crude suggestion that Michael got favorable treatment through sex
jokes.explanation: Creates the major problem for Michael and Jan. Craig's crudeness escalates the situation.

jokes.text: Surely, you cannot be serious. I am serious, and don't call me Shirley. Airplane.
jokes.setup: Tense moment with Jan
jokes.punchline: Michael quotes Airplane movie, then identifies the reference
jokes.explanation: Lazy pop culture reference with no twist. Michael identifying the source makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Surely you can't be serious.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: I am serious. And don't call me Shirley.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Did you just... that's from Airplane!

jokes.text: Women, are like wolves. If you want a wolf, you have to trap it. You have to snare it. And then you have to tame it. Keep it happy. Care for it. Feed it. Lovingly, the way an animal deserves to be loved. And my animal deserves a lot of loving.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Elaborate wolf metaphor that becomes increasingly inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight philosophy. The escalating metaphor and 'my animal' conclusion is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A successful relationship is like a wolf pack. There is an alpha male, and there is an order to things. The wolf is the most loyal creature in nature.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A man must be the wolf. He must be strong, he must be fierce, and he must always be alert.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: The female wolf, she is there to support the alpha. To nurture him. To make his den comfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: And when the wolf mounts the female from behind, it is not violent. It is tender. It is a beautiful dance of nature.

jokes.text: Let's get you home, and you are gonna get the best sex of your life.
jokes.setup: Pam wanting a romantic Valentine's gift
jokes.punchline: Roy's crude, unromantic 'solution'
jokes.explanation: Roy's complete misunderstanding of romance. Good character work showing their relationship problems.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Roy, what did you get me for Valentine's Day?

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: Nothing yet. But I was thinking, you know what would be romantic?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Roy
dialogue.line: If you got me a lap dance.

jokes.text: And Craig, you saw him, he's not the sharpest tool in the shed. Although, he is a tool.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining Craig misunderstood his joke
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'tool' meaning both implement and idiot
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with good timing. Michael's rare moment of actual wit while deflecting blame.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's not what I meant by 'tool.' I meant like a hammer or a screwdriver.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought you were calling me an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Although now that you mention it...

jokes.text: Oi vey...schmear.
jokes.setup: Michael alone after awkward Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Mixes Yiddish expression with bagel terminology
jokes.explanation: Weird Michael wordplay as episode closer. Fits his character but not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm not even upset. I'm going to go home, make myself a nice bagel with cream cheese, and I'm going to say 'oy vey' while I eat it. Because that's what sophisticated people do.

tmdb_overview: The Dunder-Mifflin staffers join in kindergarten-like festivities for Valentine's Day while Michael heads to New York City for a conference with the corporate heads. After showing the documenters a tour of his favorite city, Michael accidentally lets slip to the others at the conference that he and Jan hooked up, and he finds himself in hot water when Jan and the new CFO of Dunder-Mifflin hear the story. Back in Scranton, Pam becomes irked when she discovers that Roy hasn't gotten her anything for Valentine's Day, Dwight struggles to find a gift for Angela, and Kelley talks off Jim's ear about her rather desperate affections for Ryan, who is less than interested.
insight: Cringe-fueled romance chaos sustains 45 jokes across Valentine's Day office chaos.
